# RustFS Documentation (/)



RustFS is a high-performance, distributed object storage system written in Rust, fully compatible with the S3 protocol and released under the Apache 2.0 license. It combines simplicity with efficiency to deliver an open-source, commercially-friendly object storage solution suitable for private cloud, data lake, AI/ML, and cloud-native workloads.

## Get Started [#get-started]

* [Installation Guide](/installation/) — deploy RustFS on Linux, Windows, macOS, or Docker.
* [Quick Start on Linux](/installation/linux/quick-start) — one-click installation package.
* [What is RustFS?](/concepts/introduction) — architecture, features, and design principles.

## Explore [#explore]

* [Manage RustFS](/management/bucket/) — buckets and objects.
* [Developer Guide](/developer/) — MCP, MinIO Client, and SDKs.
* [Product Features](/features/distributed/) — distributed architecture, versioning, encryption, and more.
* [Solutions](/features/data-lake/) — data lake, AI/ML, cloud-native, and industry use cases.

## Key Features [#key-features]

* **S3 Compatible** — seamless integration with the S3 ecosystem.
* **Distributed Architecture** — scales horizontally across nodes and disks.
* **High Performance** — Rust performance with modern memory-safety guarantees.
* **Enterprise-Ready** — Apache 2.0 licensed, safe for commercial integration.
* **Cloud-Native** — optimized for Docker and Kubernetes.
